spec_match.rationale: Constructs a TCPSocket with host/port and uses the readable/writable streams — the spec's primary surface.

interactive.rationale: Form to enter host/port, send a manual request, see the streamed reply (or rejection on a normal page).

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises manual HTTP-like request via TCPSocket; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show writable.getWriter() lifecycle, AbortSignal cancellation, or what happens with backpressure when the readable buffer fills.

references.rationale: See also has no chromestatus.com link, no spec link — sibling-only. Rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, form controls follow design system.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3 self-heal: https://chromestatus.com/feature/6398297361088512 must be in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Probe AbortSignal cancellation
openQuestions.detail: TCPSocket constructor accepts a signal option. Add a 'cancel' button that aborts the in-flight connection so visitors see clean cancellation semantics.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate backpressure
openQuestions.detail: When the server emits faster than the page can read, the readable buffer fills. Show how to handle this with a slow reader / fast writer to make the streams API concrete.

summary: Real client-side TCP demo with manual request. Missing the chromestatus reference link and skips streams' lifecycle / backpressure edge cases.
